{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Turn-of-nut match marks are absent or have not been rotated, indicating lack of proper pretensioning verification."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the bolted connections under review.",
    "Instruct the installation crew to apply match marks and perform the required turn-of-nut rotation, or verify tension using an approved calibrated wrench or tension calibrator.",
    "Re-inspect the connections once the turn-of-nut method is properly executed and documented."
  ],
  "rationale": "Under the RCSC Specification, when the turn-of-nut tensioning method is specified, match-marking of the nut and bolt tip is required to verify that the necessary rotation has been achieved. The absence of these marks or the lack of rotation indicates that the bolts may not be properly pretensioned, which is a critical structural defect.",
  "references": [
    "RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts, Section 8.2.1 (Turn-of-Nut Tensioning)"
  ]
}
